Defendants Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, William E. Weiss of the law offices of William E. Weiss (415.362.6765) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Yvonne M. Pierrou of the law offices of Marion's Inn LLP (510.451.6700) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC., KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC. and IRENE SHUEN's Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action

GRANTED. This case is stayed pending conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. The court need not and does not decide whether the decedent signed the short election form in 2006 and agreed to arbitration. Even if Mr. Quach did not sign the short election form in 2006, he agreed to arbitrate in 2015. "A party's acceptance of an agreement to arbitrate may be express, as where a party signs the agreement. A signed agreement is not necessary, however, and a party's acceptance may be implied in fact." (Pinnacle Museum Tower Assn. v. Pinnacle Market Development (US), LLC (2012) 55 Cal.4th 223, 236.) Decedent suffered a heart attack in December of 2015 and the 2015 Evidence of Coverage governs the parties' relationship. Section 10 of the Evidence of Coverage states "[b]y electing coverage or accepting benefits under this Evidence of Coverage, all members legally capable of contracting, and the legal representatives of all members incapable of contracting, agree to all provisions of this Evidence of Coverage." (Emphasis in original.) Section 19 of the 2015 Evidence of Coverage provides that medical malpractice claims such as this case are to be arbitrated. Health and Safety Code 1363.1 does not bar arbitration. The 2003 amendments to the Medicare Act apply and preempt application of section 1363.1. (Drissi v. Kaiser Found. Hosps. (N.D. Cal. 2008) 543 F.Supp.2d 1076, 1080.) Plaintiffs rely on outdated authorities that examined older versions of the Medicare Act. (Clay v. Permanente Med. Group (N.D. Cal. 2007) 540 F.Supp.2d 1101, 1109-1110.) Plaintiffs are bound to arbitrate their dispute even though they are neither signatories nor parties to the arbitration agreement. The arbitration agreement unequivocally applies to decedent's heirs. Ruiz v. Podolsky (2010) 50 Cal.4th 838, 841 holds that "all wrongful death claimants are bound by arbitration agreements entered into pursuant to section 1295 [medical services arbitration], at least when'the language of the agreement manifests an intent to bind these claimants." Monschke v. Timber Ridge Assisted Living LLC (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 583 is distinguishable. In that case, the plaintiff sued an assisted living facility and the case did not implicate section 1295. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
